data.title: Spot silver falls below the $100 mark
data.description: Some trading platforms show that spot silver has fallen below the $100 mark, plunging approximately $15 during the day, and has cumulatively fallen by more than $20 from the previous day's all-time high of $121.6. (Jinshi)

data.title: Jiuzi Holdings Announces Strategic Partnership with Morgan International Finance
data.description: Jiuzi Holdings, a Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin treasury company, has announced a strategic partnership with Morgan International Finance Ltd. According to ChainCatcher, the two companies have signed a memorandum of understanding, with Morgan International Finance planning to invest $90 million through a share investment. The specific investment structure and lock-up period will be determined after further negotiations and the signing of a legally binding final agreement. The new funds are intended to support Jiuzi Holdings in expanding its blockchain and Web3 ecosystem and executing its digital asset strategy.

data.title: Gold Price Prediction for Next Week
data.description: Foresight News posted on X (formerly Twitter) about the importance of maintaining a positive mindset during challenging market conditions. The post encourages readers to engage in predicting whether gold prices will rise or fall in the coming week, offering a chance to shift focus and improve mood amidst market fluctuations.

data.title: Thailand Central Bank Addresses Baht Exchange Rate Amid U.S. Monitoring
data.description:
On January 30, Thailand's central bank announced its ability to manage the baht's exchange rate despite being placed on the U.S. watchlist for potential currency manipulation. According to Jin10, Chayawadee Chai-Anant, Assistant Governor of the Bank of Thailand, stated in Bangkok that it would be challenging for Thailand to be classified as a currency manipulator and emphasized ongoing communication with the U.S. Treasury Department.

Additionally, the central bank is tightening regulations on online gold trading to curb speculative activities that could exacerbate baht volatility. This move comes as rising gold prices and a weakening dollar have led Thai investors to sell gold, increasing foreign exchange inflows and putting upward pressure on the baht.

Under new rules effective March 1, online platforms will face stricter settlement and reporting requirements, including a ban on net settlement and short selling. Investors will be required to sell only physical gold they own, with transactions settled electronically and cash payments prohibited.

data.title: Binance Reports 2025 Achievements and Plans to Convert $100 Million SAFU Fund to Bitcoin
data.description: Binance published an open letter to the crypto community on Binance Blog, and announced that market volatility and pressures have impacted the company alongside the broader industry, reflecting the increasing complexity of the crypto ecosystem and rising demands for governance, risk management, and accountability. Throughout 2025, Binance focused on risk control, compliance cooperation, and ecosystem development: assisting users in recovering 38,648 incorrect deposits totaling $48 million, with cumulative recoveries exceeding $1.09 billion; helping 5.4 million users identify potential risks and preventing approximately $6.69 billion in scam-related losses; collaborating with global law enforcement agencies to confiscate $131 million in illicit funds. Binance’s spot listing program covered 21 public blockchains, including 13 newly launched chains spanning payment, gaming, social, and other use cases. By the end of 2025, Binance’s proof-of-reserves demonstrated full backing of user assets totaling approximately $162.8 billion across 45 crypto assets. Binance remains committed to responding to market concerns through concrete actions and promoting sustainable industry growth. The company also announced plans to convert its $100 million SAFU fund stablecoin reserves into Bitcoin within 30 days of the announcement, with a rebalancing mechanism to maintain the fund’s value above $80 million amid Bitcoin price fluctuations. Binance will continue to uphold transparency, openness, and long-term commitment to advancing the crypto industry.

data.title: OKX, in partnership with Cantina, officially launched an on-chain bug bounty program with a total prize pool of $1 million.
data.description: OKX, in partnership with security platform Cantina, officially launched the OKX On-Chain Contract Vulnerability Bounty Program, with a total prize pool of $1 million. This program focuses on on-chain smart contracts already deployed on the mainnet and operating in real production environments, currently offering security reviews and bounties for DEX routing and related contracts that actually handle user asset transfers. OKX currently runs production-grade on-chain DEX routing infrastructure across multiple ecosystems, situated in critical execution paths for user on-chain transactions. By introducing a continuous, structured, and transparent vulnerability review and bounty mechanism, OKX aims to further enhance the security and transparency of its on-chain trading systems, setting a standard for production-grade on-chain system security maintenance in the industry.

data.title: Jiuzi Holdings receives $90 million in investment to advance its blockchain and Web3 ecosystem expansion.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin treasury company Jiuzi Holdings announced a strategic cooperation memorandum of understanding with Morgan International Finance Ltd., under which the latter plans to invest $90 million in Jiuzi through equity investment. Specific investment structures, lock-up periods, and other arrangements will be determined after further negotiation and the signing of a legally binding final transaction document. The new funds will be used to support Jiuzi Holdings' expansion into the blockchain and Web3 ecosystem, as well as the execution of its digital asset strategy. (PRNewswire)

data.title: The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) has added "R-Coin Wallet/R-Wallet/Jumanjin" to its list of suspicious virtual asset trading platforms.
data.description: The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) announced today that it has added "R-Coin Wallet/R-Wallet/聚满金" to its list of suspicious virtual asset trading platforms. The SFC stated that the entity claims to operate a virtual asset trading platform and is suspected of engaging in fraudulent activities related to virtual assets. In addition, the entity falsely claims to be a virtual asset trading platform licensed by the SFC and has misused the central entity number of a licensed virtual asset trading platform, but the entity has no connection with the relevant licensed virtual asset trading platform.

data.title: Hong Kong Securities Regulator Flags Suspicious Virtual Asset Platform
data.description: The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) has officially announced the inclusion of 'R-Coin Wallet/R-Wallet/聚满金' in its list of suspicious virtual asset trading platforms. According to Odaily, the SFC stated that the entity claims to operate a virtual asset trading platform and is suspected of engaging in fraudulent activities related to virtual assets. Furthermore, the entity falsely claims to be a licensed virtual asset trading platform by the Hong Kong SFC and has misused the central entity number of a licensed virtual asset trading platform. However, there is no connection between this entity and the licensed virtual asset trading platform.

data.title: Data: In January, the Solana network had over 5 million active addresses, and daily transaction volume jumped to 87 million.
data.description: Nansen released Solana's performance data for the first 30 days of 2026 on the X platform, revealing that Solana's number of active addresses doubled to over 5 million, daily transaction volume jumped from 52 million to 87 million, and transaction fees exceeded $1.1 million.

data.title: Spot gold and silver prices continued their decline, with gold plunging $350 and silver falling below the $101 mark.
data.description: Spot gold and silver prices continued their decline. Spot gold plunged $350 intraday, currently trading at $5020.64 per ounce, a drop of 6.52%. Spot silver fell below $101 per ounce, down 12.53% intraday. New York silver futures plunged 12.00% intraday, currently trading at $100.64 per ounce. (Jinshi)

data.title: Significant SOL Transfer Between Anonymous Addresses
data.description: A substantial transfer of SOL tokens has been reported. According to ChainCatcher, at 16:41, a total of 45,004 SOL, valued at approximately $5.19 million, was moved from one anonymous address, beginning with 3wuiZRhQ, to another anonymous address, starting with 8wE8f86.

data.title: Ethereum State Expiry Performance Experiment Reveals Significant Improvements
data.description:
Ethereum execution layer researcher weiihann has released a performance experiment report on state expiry. According to Foresight News, the experiment involved running approximately one year of Ethereum mainnet block load on the Geth client, comparing the performance differences between 'full state nodes' and 'nodes retaining only one year of active state.' The study found that reducing state size not only lowers hardware requirements but also provides room for increasing gas limits and throughput.

The experiment results indicate that retaining only active states accessed within one year reduces the database size from 359 GB to 81 GB, a decrease of about 77.5%, with the most significant reduction observed in storing Trie nodes. In terms of performance, block re-execution time was shortened by approximately 15%, and read performance improved significantly, particularly with storage read P50 latency reduced by 46% and P99 latency by 36%. Additionally, tail latency improved, with P99 block insertion time reduced by 21%, aiding nodes in maintaining synchronization under load. Future research will explore comparisons with other clients, different expiry cycles such as six months, and strategies focused solely on contract storage cleanup.

data.title: India's Government Bond Lending Plan Faces Delays Due to Tax Uncertainty
data.description:
India's plan to allow government bond lending remains stalled despite the necessary infrastructure being ready for over 18 months, according to informed sources. The delay is attributed to uncertainties surrounding the applicable tax regime. According to Jin10, since the establishment of a trading platform by the country's sovereign debt clearing house in July 2024, no transactions have been completed. Market participants are awaiting clarity on whether the lender or borrower of the bonds will be responsible for paying the Goods and Services Tax (GST).

The Reserve Bank of India announced this initiative in February 2023, aiming to provide investors, such as insurance companies—the second-largest holders of government bonds—with a means to leverage their substantial bond holdings and enhance market liquidity. Sources indicate that the central concern is whether the 'reverse charge mechanism' used in stock lending, where the borrower pays the tax instead of the lender, will be applied. Authorities and market participants have discussed whether the same terms should apply to bonds, but no final decision has been made yet.

data.title: With funding from the US Senate stalled, Polymarket predicts the probability of a US government shutdown on Saturday has risen to 66%.
data.description: According to Polymarket data, as of now, the market estimates the probability of a US government shutdown this Saturday at approximately 66%, a significant increase from previous estimates. Data shows that the forecast contract fluctuated more sharply following news that the US Senate failed to advance an appropriations bill; the shutdown probability initially surged before falling back to its current level, with a cumulative trading volume of approximately $28.96 million.

data.title: Japan's Q4 Economic Growth Expected to Show Modest Recovery
data.description: Japan's economy is anticipated to experience a modest recovery in the fourth quarter, according to a report by ING analyst Min Joo Kang. According to Jin10, the slight rebound is expected to be driven primarily by private consumption and the services sector, despite a slowdown in monthly economic activity. The senior economist for the Korea-Japan region at the bank noted that retail sales in December fell more than expected, and manufacturing activity likely remained sluggish during the quarter. Japan's GDP is projected to grow by 0.2% quarter-on-quarter in the fourth quarter of 2025, reversing a 0.6% contraction in the third quarter.

data.title: Gold prices fell sharply as the market focused on the nomination of the Federal Reserve Chair.
data.description: Gold prices retreated sharply after surging to record highs as traders took profits and a stronger dollar made it more expensive for overseas buyers to purchase the precious metal. Market focus has now shifted to Trump's expected decision on his nominee for Federal Reserve chair, due later on Friday. Trump's advisors have hinted that he may nominate Kevin Warsh. Saxo Bank analysts noted that Warsh is considered less controversial and less clearly dovish compared to other candidates circulating before the nomination announcement. (Jinshi)

data.title: DOJ Completes Forfeiture of $400 Million in Crypto Linked to Helix
data.description:
The United States Department of Justice (DOJ) has finalized the forfeiture of over $400 million in cryptocurrency and other assets associated with Helix, a Bitcoin-era darknet mixing service. According to Cointelegraph, the assets were seized from Larry Harmon, the operator of Helix, which facilitated transactions between 2014 and 2017. The crypto mixer was designed to obscure the origins and destinations of Bitcoin linked to darknet markets.

The forfeiture follows a January 21 order by the US District Court for the District of Columbia, which formally transferred ownership of the assets to the government. This final court order grants the government legal title to the seized digital assets, real estate, and financial assets connected to Helix's operations. This development marks the conclusion of one of the most significant early Bitcoin mixer prosecutions in US courts, highlighting the lengthy process of resolving major crypto-related enforcement actions, even after criminal activities have ceased.

Helix processed at least 354,468 Bitcoin during its operation, valued at approximately $300 million at the time of the transactions, according to the DOJ. Prosecutors linked this activity to darknet drug markets aiming to launder illegal proceeds. Harmon also operated Grams, a search engine designed to support major darknet markets active during that period. Investigators revealed that Helix's application programming interface allowed marketplaces to integrate the mixer directly into their Bitcoin withdrawal systems, facilitating large-scale laundering activities. The DOJ reported that investigators traced tens of millions from darknet markets to Helix.

Harmon was arrested in February 2020 and pleaded guilty in August 2021 to conspiracy to commit money laundering. He was sentenced in November 2024 to three years in prison. At the time, the court ordered Harmon to forfeit assets valued at more than $400 million. Last week's order finalized the forfeiture, transferring clear legal title to the US government. As previously reported by Cointelegraph, Harmon's sentence was reduced after he cooperated with investigators, including testifying in the Bitcoin Fog case against Roman Sterlingov.

data.title: U.S. Missile Destroyer Docks at Israel's Red Sea Port
data.description: On January 30, a U.S. missile destroyer arrived at the Israeli Red Sea port of Eilat. According to Jin10, the Israeli military stated that the arrival of the destroyer was pre-planned and is part of ongoing cooperation between the Israel Defense Forces and the U.S. military.
